reason: Firebase Hosting cache directives are managed in firebase.json and reviewed separately.
Firebase Authentication SDK scripts are intentionally loaded from Google's CDN.
Reviewed source comments in static JavaScript do not disclose secrets or operational credentials.
TLS termination and HSTS for Hosting and rewritten Function responses are platform-managed.
Public static pages and aggregate responses are intentionally cacheable.
Firebase Hosting is expected to serve public static pages from cache.
The deployed CSP is explicit for this static site and permits the required Firebase services.
The site uses JavaScript progressively and is expected to be identified as a modern web application.
Forms use server-side origin, authentication, validation, and rate-limit controls instead of synchronizer tokens.
Versioned Firebase SDK modules are intentionally loaded from Google's CDN without fixed SRI hashes.
Cross-origin isolation is incompatible with the required Firebase Authentication SDK resources.
